title: Italian police conduct raids on several luxury brands in labour abuse probe
content: At issue is the near-ubiquitous practice of brands subcontracting work to suppliers that in turn contract to others, ending up with firms who employ Chinese workers in exploitative conditions.
snippet: At issue is the near-ubiquitous practice of brands subcontracting work to suppliers that in turn contract to others, ending up with firms who employ Chinese workers in exploitative conditions.

title: EU orders Google to share search data, open Android to AI rivals competitors
content: Brussels said Google must start sharing search data from January 2027 and users would start benefitting from the changes to Android from July 2027.
snippet: Brussels said Google must start sharing search data from January 2027 and users would start benefitting from the changes to Android from July 2027.

title: Ex-CEO of Italian motorway operator among 32 convicted over Genoa bridge collapse
content: Dozens of family members of the victims packed the courtroom as Chief Judge Paolo Lepri read the verdicts against 57 defendants. Many relatives broke down in tears as the sentences were read.
snippet: Dozens of family members of the victims packed the courtroom as Chief Judge Paolo Lepri read the verdicts against 57 defendants. Many relatives broke down in tears as the sentences were read.

title: Storm-1516: Fake Hezbollah video threatening Bastille Day attack possibly Russia linked
content: A social media video emerged ahead of this year's Bastille Day in which alleged Hezbollah militants threatened attacks on French soil. The video turned out to be fake, and researchers say it could be linked to a well-known Russian disinformation campaign.

title: Kubilius hails 'revolutionary' defence plan, but MEPs demand faster rollout
content: The European Commissioner for Defence says Europe's military mobility overhaul is necessary, but MEPs are pushing for an accelerated timeline.
snippet: The European Commissioner for Defence says Europe's military mobility overhaul is necessary, but MEPs are pushing for an accelerated timeline.

title: Zelenskyy dismisses Ukraine’s popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov
content: “It was a great honour to serve the Ukrainian people as defence minister," Fedorov said on Wednesday evening, confirming his dismissal amid Zelenskyy’s government reshuffle. While not a very public figure, Fedorov has been widely praised within the military and among civil society.

title: Can common debt fix Europe’s growth problem? MEPs debate in The Ring
content: In this week’s episode of The Ring, EU lawmakers Markus Ferber and Pasquale Tridico clash over whether common debt is the answer to Europe's stagnant economy and weakened competitiveness.
snippet: In this week’s episode of The Ring, EU lawmakers Markus Ferber and Pasquale Tridico clash over whether common debt is the answer to Europe's stagnant economy and weakened competitiveness.

title: French parliament passes assisted dying bill for some terminally ill adults
content: After years of debate, French MPs have voted to adopt a bill to establish a right to assisted dying for some adults suffering from an incurable condition.
snippet: After years of debate, French MPs have voted to adopt a bill to establish a right to assisted dying for some adults suffering from an incurable condition.

title: Greenland research centre halts new collaborations with US to protect its scientists
content: The Trump administration has cut billions of dollars in public research funding and fired government scientists.
snippet: The Trump administration has cut billions of dollars in public research funding and fired government scientists.

title: Former Hungarian foreign minister Szijjártó resigns from parliament to join Chinese carmaker
content: Péter Szijjártó announced his resignation and details of his new position with the world's largest electric carmaker, China's BYD, on his Facebook page on Wednesday, detailing that he'll be filling an "international executive position".

title: Spain ratifies Gibraltar deal but insists no change in sovereignty claims over the territory
content: The new deal officially brings to an end the final chapter of the UK's exit process from the EU in a deal set to boost cooperation and freedoms in the disputed territory.
snippet: The new deal officially brings to an end the final chapter of the UK's exit process from the EU in a deal set to boost cooperation and freedoms in the disputed territory.

title: National sovereignty equals 'sending gifts to US and China', former Italian PM Letta tells Euronews
content: The former Italian prime minister tells Euronews that, to be able to compete with Washington and Beijing, the EU must keep a united front, adding that "the true battle is for European sovereignty".
snippet: The former Italian prime minister tells Euronews that, to be able to compete with Washington and Beijing, the EU must keep a united front, adding that "the true battle is for European sovereignty".

content: Hungary's parliament has approved a constitutional amendment to remove President Tamás Sulyok from office, with Prime Minister Péter Magyar accusing him of being Orbán's puppet. But he refuses to resign and argues the move in unconstitutional. What happens next? Euronews explains:

title: Hungary risks missing early Erasmus+ return despite EU promises
content: Budapest may miss the September deadline to rejoin the programme unless it submits rule-of-law reforms to Brussels on schedule.
snippet: Budapest may miss the September deadline to rejoin the programme unless it submits rule-of-law reforms to Brussels on schedule.

title: Several reported dead after fire in lift shaft of Brussels construction site
content: Local media reports say firefighters initially brought the blaze under control, but flames spread into a lift shaft, sparking a second fire and trapping people inside.
snippet: Local media reports say firefighters initially brought the blaze under control, but flames spread into a lift shaft, sparking a second fire and trapping people inside.

title: Summer skiers head to melting glacier as heatwave grips France
content: Hundreds of skiers headed to the Grande Motte glacier in Tignes to escape France's heatwave and enjoy summer skiing. But visitors and experts say the glacier is shrinking rapidly, with scientists warning it could disappear within 10 to 15 years.

title: Hungarian parliament removes President Tamás Sulyok from office
content: Hungary's parliament has approved the 17th amendment to the constitution. Prime Mİnister Péter Magyar says this is a key step that will allow 'Orbán's political puppets' to be replaced.
snippet: Hungary's parliament has approved the 17th amendment to the constitution. Prime Mİnister Péter Magyar says this is a key step that will allow 'Orbán's political puppets' to be replaced.

title: Full ban on Israeli settlement trade gets ‘most support’ from EU countries, Kallas says
content: EU foreign ministers overwhelmingly backed clamping down on EU trade with Israeli settlements during closed-door discussions on Monday, with an official source saying the biggest cohort of countries supported passing new measures without requiring the unanimous backing of all member states.

title: UK joins EU's €90 billion loan for Ukraine with promise of 'fair' contribution
content: London has agreed to join in the long-in-the-making EU plan to give Ukraine a much-needed financial boost.
snippet: London has agreed to join in the long-in-the-making EU plan to give Ukraine a much-needed financial boost.

title: Nine European countries and Ukraine form anti-ballistic missile coalition
content: The creation of the anti-ballistic missile coalition comes in the wake of a growing threat of ballistic missiles on Europe. The declaration states the coalition serves in a 'purely defensive' manner.
snippet: The creation of the anti-ballistic missile coalition comes in the wake of a growing threat of ballistic missiles on Europe. The declaration states the coalition serves in a 'purely defensive' manner.

title: UK court jails two men over major cyberattack on London's transport network
content: TfL, which had to reset the passwords of around 27,000 employees, estimated the attack cost the organisation around £29 million (€34 million) in damages and a further £10 million (€11.7 million) in lost income.

content: In total, charges were brought against 22 defendants, while proceedings against seven serving MPs were shelved for lack of sufficient evidence.
snippet: In total, charges were brought against 22 defendants, while proceedings against seven serving MPs were shelved for lack of sufficient evidence.

title: Exclusive: US wants ‘a new approach’ to rebuild Gaza, Kushner told EU ministers

title: Dismissed defence minister Fedorov accuses Ukraine's army chief of fuelling divisions, incompetence
content: Ukraine's dismissed Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov accused Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi of obstructing military reform and "splitting the country", telling reporters in a bombshell press conference that President  Zelenskyy had chosen to keep Syrskyi in the post over him.

title: China is 'a critical long-term strategic challenge', EU ministers say

title: Ukrainian parliament backs new wartime cabinet, amid scandal over defence minister dismissal
content: As thousands protested the dismissal of defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov, lawmakers approved almost the entire new cabinet, making Sergii Koretskyi prime minister — a far less contentious step than the shake‑up at the defence ministry.

title: Fact check: False claims target French referee after Egypt World Cup exit
content: Screenshots of temporary edits to François Letexier's Wikipedia page have been used to support claims that Egypt's match against Argentina was rigged.
snippet: Screenshots of temporary edits to François Letexier's Wikipedia page have been used to support claims that Egypt's match against Argentina was rigged.

title: Fifteen homes for the price of a city flat in Germany's mini-village
content: In Thuringia, Germany, an entire mini-village is up for sale for €390,000, including 15 buildings, but the dream comes with a catch.

title: 'Marine Le Pen is France's last hope', says Musk amid interference claims
content: In a post on X on Wednesday, Elon Musk backed Marine Le Pen ahead of France’s presidential vote, prompting claims of foreign interference and electoral risk.
snippet: In a post on X on Wednesday, Elon Musk backed Marine Le Pen ahead of France’s presidential vote, prompting claims of foreign interference and electoral risk.

title: EU Court of Justice clears way for Puigdemont amnesty as it backs Spain
content: The ECJ backs Spain’s amnesty law, saying it breaches neither EU nor anti-terror rules, although Puigdemont's arrest warrant stands until the Constitutional Court decides on his appeal.
snippet: The ECJ backs Spain’s amnesty law, saying it breaches neither EU nor anti-terror rules, although Puigdemont's arrest warrant stands until the Constitutional Court decides on his appeal.

title: Ukrainians hold rare wartime protest over defence minister’s dismissal
content: For the first time since last summer’s protests in support of their country's anti‑corruption watchdogs, Ukrainians have mobilised online over the dismissal of Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov. A wave of social media posts quickly turned into calls to gather across Ukrainian cities on Thursday.

title: Chat Control is 'treating everyone as a suspect', says expert
content: Simeon de Brouwer, policy adviser at digital rights organisation EDRi, calls for "proportionality" to avoid "mass surveillance" and "undermining everyone's privacy" under the proposed Chat Control law to tackle online child sexual abuse. He advocates for "targeted scanning".

title: Starmer tells Ukraine UK support will 'endure' after his departure

title: Education comes in different forms: How do Europeans feel about vocational training?
content: A majority of EU citizens value general education more than vocational training, but 80% agree that the latter leads to in-demand jobs amid perceived skill shortages.
snippet: A majority of EU citizens value general education more than vocational training, but 80% agree that the latter leads to in-demand jobs amid perceived skill shortages.

content: Brussels, member states, industry, and banks have signed the EU's first Tripartite Agreement for Energy Storage to address a significant capacity gap, essential for lower energy costs and grid stability. title: Europe Today: Middle East tensions rise as Europe deepens support for Ukraine
content: US strikes on Iran escalate tensions in the Middle East as fears grow over the Strait of Hormuz and global energy markets. In Kyiv, the EU and Ukraine deepen defence cooperation with a new drone partnership, while Europe debates its role in supporting Ukraine’s war effort.

title: Lithuanian and Latvian leaders warn Russia planning infrastructure attacks on Baltics or Poland
content: The warnings are the latest in a series of alarms from leaders along NATO's eastern flank who suggest Russia may be trying to test the alliance as the Ukraine war drags on after more than four years.

title: Keir Starmer bids UK MPs 'goodbye', vows to support Burnham

snippet: Andy Burnham is to replace Starmer on Monday, shortly after he is expected to meet King Charles III to be asked to form the next government.

title: EU pushes US to exempt €150 billion worth of EU goods from Turnberry deal
content: Brussels is seeking carve-outs from the 15 percent tariffs imposed by Washington on EU goods under the trade agreement reached in Scotland in 2025. The list, obtained by Euronews, includes iconic products such as Roquefort cheese, olive oil and wine.

title: EU extends temporary protection for Ukrainians but restricts access for military-age men
content: Most Ukrainian men over 23 are prevented from leaving the country while it mobilises its military-age population en masse to fight Russia's full-scale invasion.

title: Fact check: US military programme not behind Europe's recent heatwaves
content: As Europe experiences another spell of extreme heat, social media users are once again claiming that a US military programme is manipulating the weather and causing the continent's high temperatures. Available evidence does not support the claim.
snippet: As Europe experiences another spell of extreme heat, social media users are once again claiming that a US military programme is manipulating the weather and causing the continent's high temperatures.

title: Cypriot minister defends appointment of EU Cyprus special representative following backlash

title: Where is the EU's population growing the most?
content: Some of the EU's smallest countries have the highest rates of population growth, while its biggest members account for more than two-thirds of the bloc's population over all.
snippet: Some of the EU's smallest countries have the highest rates of population growth, while its biggest members account for more than two-thirds of the bloc's population over all.

title: 'Food insecurity is on the increase', World Food Programme Director says
content: Conflict in Lebanon has "hit humanitarian operations" worldwide, says WFP Executive Director Carl Skau. "In the poorest countries, people are now eating less" as rising costs have reduced aid deliveries and disrupted supply chains.

title: Why Jared Kushner is crucial in the EU plan for Gaza

title: Spanish PM Pedro Sánchez says Gibraltar border removal closes 'open wound'
content: Gibraltar, a self-governing British territory at the southernmost tip of the Iberian peninsula, is home to only about 40,000 people but relies on some 15,500 workers who cross from Spain every day.

title: Europe Today: Ukraine at a turning point as Europe steps up support
content: Ukraine is facing a political reset as President Volodymyr Zelenskyy reshuffles his government amid intensified Russian attacks. The EU prepares fresh support for Kyiv as Ursula von der Leyen visits, while food security, Gibraltar’s post-Brexit future and Cyprus tensions remain in focus.

title: Can batteries save the EU's wasted renewable energy?
content: The EU's first tripartite agreement on energy storage, signed in June, puts batteries at the heart of plans to capture more solar and wind power. The technology is ready and getting cheaper, but market rules, grid delays, industrial policy and raw-material politics still hold it back.

title: Former politician Ann Widdecombe was killed in 'targeted attack,' UK police say
content: Devon and Cornwall Police have been criticised for originally saying the killing was not believed to be a terror-related crime and there was nothing to suggest it was politically motivated.
snippet: Devon and Cornwall Police have been criticised for originally saying the killing was not believed to be a terror-related crime and there was nothing to suggest it was politically motivated.

title: Spain confirms 12 foreigners among 13 victims of Los Gallardos wildfire as missing toll rises
content: Flames fanned by high winds ripped through forests and scrubland made tinderbox dry by extreme high temperatures, turning picturesque rural settlements into ghost towns.
snippet: Flames fanned by high winds ripped through forests and scrubland made tinderbox dry by extreme high temperatures, turning picturesque rural settlements into ghost towns.

title: June was deadliest month for Ukraine civilians in over four years, UN monitors say
content: In total, the UN has recorded 16,431 verified civilian deaths in Ukraine since Russia invaded in February 2022, among them 803 children.
snippet: In total, the UN has recorded 16,431 verified civilian deaths in Ukraine since Russia invaded in February 2022, among them 803 children.
